title: brain git reject <runId> — --json success output
description: Terminal rejection of an open run: transitions the run to the terminal rejected state, removes its worktree, and emits exactly one run.rejected ledger event. Per recovery-state-machine.md `rejected`, the agent-branch commit is RETAINED (not deleted) for audit / possible re-plan — only the worktree is torn down; the branch commit survives. Shared, intrinsically idempotent by runId (re-rejecting a rejected run is a no-op success). Never touches canonical history. Exit 0 on rejection; exit 1 when the run does not exist or is not rejectable.

worktreeRemoved.description: Mandatory completion postcondition: the run's worktree was removed. The agent branch commit is NOT removed.

retainedCommit.description: 40-char hex agent-branch commit retained for audit / re-plan (recovery-state-machine.md `rejected`: the commit is kept, never fast-forwarded onto canonical).

x-atlas-contract.summary: Terminal reject of an open run + worktree removal; RETAINS the agent-branch commit for audit; emits run.rejected; idempotent by runId.
sideEffects: retains the agent-branch commit (refs/agent/<runId>) for audit / re-plan
prohibitedEffects: never deletes the agent-branch commit — it is retained for audit / re-plan (recovery-state-machine.md `rejected`)
never emits more than one run.rejected event per run

errorCodes.when: the run is in a terminal non-rejectable state
the vault repo is missing or unreadable
the fail-closed backup watermark blocks a ledger-writing run
